symptoms.symptom_name: Postprandial epigastric burning discomfort, worst 30-60 minutes after eating

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Meal-related epigastric burning is a core dyspepsia feature (epigastric pain syndrome overlap).
Burning discomfort and nocturnal symptoms also fit reflux; empirical acid suppression treats both.
Early satiation and postprandial fullness define the postprandial distress syndrome subtype of functional dyspepsia.
Bloating and eructation accompany impaired gastric accommodation in functional dyspepsia.
Mild nausea is common and non-specific; absence of vomiting argues against obstruction or a severe acute abdomen.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of GI-bleeding alarm features (no haematemesis, coffee-ground vomiting or melena)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No overt bleeding lowers the probability of a complicated ulcer and supports safe empirical outpatient management.
Absence of bleeding, dysphagia and weight loss makes malignancy unlikely and removes the indication for urgent endoscopy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of dysphagia and unintentional weight loss

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Dysphagia and weight loss are the highest-yield alarm features; their absence strongly supports a benign course.

symptoms.symptom_name: Non-cardiac character — pain strictly food-related and reproducible, not exertional, no radiation, no diaphoresis or dyspnoea

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reproducible meal-related pain with no exertional link, radiation or autonomic features makes an anginal equivalent very unlikely — screenable entirely by history in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Epigastric tenderness on deep palpation, no guarding/rebound/rigidity, Murphy's sign negative

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Negative Murphy's sign and absence of peritonism argue against biliary and surgical pathology — a clinician-measured finding sealed here; the AI Doctor infers benignity from the history, not a measured examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal vital signs (afebrile, normotensive, not tachycardic, SpO2 normal)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal observations argue against sepsis or an acute surgical/cardiac emergency — clinician-measured and sealed; not available to the AI Doctor without a home device.
A positive H. pylori test reclassifies management toward eradication; test-and-treat is the guideline pathway. Not available in real time in telehealth — arranged via the GP.
